title: Probability scoring — India VIX + global macro context (US VIX, S&P 500, DAX, crude oil, gold) + time/day adjustments
acceptance_criteria: scoreProbability(input: ScoringInput) → ScoringResult exported as a pure function with no I/O. All context is passed in as parameters — no Redis reads inside this function.

ScoringInput type: { rawExhaustionScore: number; signalType: 'MOMENTUM_EXHAUSTION'|'SCHEDULED'|'PULLBACK'; indiaVix: number|null; macro: MacroContext; signalTimeMs: number; clock: Clock } — MacroContext imported from src/ingestion/global-macro-feed.ts (T-65)

ScoringResult type: { rawProbability: number; adjustedProbability: number; confidenceTier: 'HIGH'|'MEDIUM'|'LOW'; adjustmentBreakdown: Record<string, number> } — breakdown is for logging/debugging signal quality
rawProbability = baseProbability mapped from rawExhaustionScore: exhaustionScore * 0.40 + 0.35 (score 0→prob 0.35, score 1→prob 0.75)
INDIA VIX adjustment (indiaVix parameter): null → 0; <= 15 → +0.02; 15–25 → -(indiaVix-15)*0.005; > 25 → -0.05 fixed. Cap at -0.10. This is the primary volatility input.
US VIX adjustment (macro.us_vix): null → 0; us_vix.value < 15 → +0.02 (US complacency, benign); 20–30 → -0.04; > 30 → -0.08 (global fear spike). US VIX > 30 is the strongest downward signal.

S&P 500 adjustment (macro.sp500.change_pct): null → 0; down > 1.5% → -0.06 (strong global risk-off); down 0.5–1.5% → -0.03; up > 1.5% → +0.03 (global risk-on tends to calm Indian markets). Uses today's % change from previous close.

DAX adjustment (macro.dax.change_pct): null → 0; down > 1.5% → -0.04; down 0.5–1.5% → -0.02; up > 1.5% → +0.02. European session leads Indian afternoon — relevant for 13:00–15:00 IST window especially.

Crude oil adjustment (macro.crude_oil.change_pct): null → 0; |change_pct| > 3% → -0.05 (high crude volatility creates uncertainty for India as oil importer, regardless of direction); |change_pct| 1.5–3% → -0.02; |change_pct| < 1.5% → 0. Use absolute value — both spike and crash are destabilising.

Gold adjustment (macro.gold.change_pct): null → 0; gold up > 2% → -0.05 (strong safe-haven demand signals global risk-off); up 1–2% → -0.03; gold down or flat → 0. Gold decline is neutral (not positive) — falling gold in risk-on environments is already captured by S&P 500 positive adjustment.
Time-of-day adjustment: 09:20–09:45 IST → +0.05 (early session premium decay phase); 14:00–15:00 IST → -0.04 (low liquidity, wide spreads); else 0
Day-of-week adjustment: Monday → -0.03 (weekend gap risk); Friday → -0.03 (expiry week, lower confidence in exhaustion); else 0
adjustedProbability = clamp(rawProbability + indiaVixAdj + usVixAdj + sp500Adj + daxAdj + crudeOilAdj + goldAdj + timeAdj + dowAdj, 0.0, 1.0)

adjustmentBreakdown in ScoringResult records each named adjustment value (e.g. {india_vix: -0.03, us_vix: -0.04, sp500: 0, dax: -0.02, crude_oil: -0.02, gold: 0, time_of_day: +0.05, day_of_week: -0.03}) for signal quality logging

SCHEDULED signals: fixed adjustedProbability = 0.60, rawProbability = 0.60, no macro adjustments applied. Rationale: Clockwork's fixed-time entry is not a quality signal — it is a decision to enter regardless of conditions. Applying macro penalties would prevent Clockwork from ever trading on bad global days, which defeats its purpose as a naive benchmark.
PULLBACK signals: same adjustment formula as MOMENTUM_EXHAUSTION but baseProbability = 0.60 (slightly lower confidence than a fresh exhaustion signal since it is a secondary detection)

Unit tests cover: clamp to [0,1] on extreme inputs; each individual adjustment correct direction and magnitude; SCHEDULED always 0.60 regardless of inputs; all-null MacroContext produces same result as zero macro adjustments; PULLBACK uses 0.60 base; adjustmentBreakdown keys all present even when adjustment is 0
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
